description: This group of optical micrographs shows the microstructures of Fe-Mn-Al-C system austenitic steels fabricated via jet spraying rapid solidification, hot pressing at 1150 °C, and quenching from 1100 °C, with a uniform scale bar of 30 μm for both subgraphs. Subgraph (a) presents the microstructure of the sample with 9.8 wt% Al, where no δ-ferrite is observed, consisting of austenite grains with acicular and strip-like morphologies. Subgraph (b) shows the sample with 14 wt% Al, where polygonal austenite grains are dominant, and around 10 vol.% δ-ferrite (marked with an arrow) is detected at grain boundary regions. The comparison verifies that rapid solidification can suppress δ-ferrite formation when Al content is no higher than 12 wt%, while excess Al (14 wt%) leads to δ-ferrite precipitation, which supports the composition optimization strategy to avoid embrittling phases for good mechanical properties of the steel.
